title: Replace monolithic docs workflow with staged deployment system
body:
- [x] Remove old `.github/workflows/builds-docs.yml`
- [x] Create merged `docs-deploy.yml`, `docs-cleanup-staging-pr.yml`, `docs-go-live.yml`
- [x] Fix `docs/docfx.json` — `net9.0` → `net10.0`
- [x] Add `docs-deploy-fork-pr.yml` — secure fork PR staging via `workflow_run`
- [x] Security hardening — least-privilege permissions, env vars, PR number validation
- [x] Review feedback: PR_NUMBER validation, dedup comment, cleanup gh-pages guard, YAML parse fix
- [x] Build info via `AssemblyMetadata` MSBuild attribute + `MainLayout.razor` runtime read
- [x] Fix: restore missing `@media (min-width: 641px) {` in `MainLayout.razor.css`

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

💡 You can make Copilot smarter by setting up custom instructions, customizing its development environment and configuring Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. Learn more [Copilot coding agent tips](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips) in the docs.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
@copilot can you see if you can create a local composite action that runs the build so we don't need to duplicate the steps in the main vs staging. Or maybe see if there is a way to merge the deploy workflow and use different params/vars depending. 

Please try the merge first as that is the preferred way

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot can you see if you can create a local composite action that runs the build so we don't need to duplicate the steps in the main vs staging. Or maybe see if there is a way to merge the deploy w...

Done in 7bdb9e1. Merged `docs-deploy-main.yml` and `docs-deploy-staging-pr.yml` into a single `docs-deploy.yml`.

A `setup-build-variables` step at the start of the `build` job branches on `github.event_name` to emit outputs (`artifact_name`, `sample_base_href`, `sample_segment_count`, `is_staging`, `pr_number`). The rest of the job consumes those outputs, so the build steps are written once. In the `deploy` job, two conditional steps replace the two separate deploy jobs — one runs when `is_staging == 'false'` (updates gh-pages root, preserving `staging/`), the other when `is_staging == 'true'` (writes to `staging/{pr}/`). The `comment` job condition now also guards on `is_staging == 'true'`. `docs-go-live.yml` is updated to reference the new `"Docs - Deploy"` workflow name.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: @copilot can we use the Ubuntu-latest for the docs. Then also switch to use the Maui-android workload.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot can we use the Ubuntu-latest for the docs. Then also switch to use the Maui-android workload. 

Done in 89984da. The build job now runs on `ubuntu-latest` with `dotnet workload install maui-android`. The PowerShell copy/rewrite step was also converted to bash to match the new Linux runner.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: @copilot please review the workflow changes and let me know what issues there are. Also, please visit the staging links to make sure it all works.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot please review the workflow changes and let me know what issues there are. Also, please visit the staging links to make sure it all works. 

Review complete. One issue found and fixed (ea0c18f):

**Fixed — Fork PR deploy would fail**: The `deploy` job has `permissions: contents: write` and does `git push origin gh-pages`. For PRs from forked repos, `GITHUB_TOKEN` is read-only and the push would fail. Added a job-level `if` guard: `github.event_name != 'pull_request' || github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository`. The `comment` job is naturally skipped when `deploy` is skipped (via `needs`).

**Not fixed — pre-existing**: `docfx.json` looks for `**/bin/Release/net9.0/**.dll` but the build step uses `-f net10.0`. This existed in the original `builds-docs.yml` and docfx silently skips missing metadata sources. Separately tracked.

**Staging links**: The `docs-deploy.yml` workflow runs on this PR itself (it matches the path filter `.github/workflows/docs-deploy.yml`). I wasn't able to reach the live staging URLs from my environment, so staging verification will need to be confirmed once the CI run on commit ea0c18f completes.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot please address the review comments 

All 4 review comments addressed in 8897fca:

1. **PR_NUMBER validation in staging deploy** (`docs-deploy.yml` line 146) — added `[[ "$PR_NUMBER" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]]` regex guard before filesystem operations, matching the fork PR workflow pattern.

2. **PR_NUMBER validation in cleanup** (`docs-cleanup-staging-pr.yml`) — same regex guard added before any path use.

3. **Duplicate comment on reopen** (`docs-deploy.yml` comment job) — added `listComments` check for existing bot comment with "Documentation Preview" body, identical to the fork PR workflow's dedup logic.

4. **Silent wrong-branch operation in cleanup** — merged the two separate checkout steps into one. The `git fetch` and `git checkout` are now tested with `if !` and exit cleanly with a clear message if gh-pages doesn't exist, rather than falling through to operate on the default branch.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
@copilot there are now yaml errors:

[Invalid workflow file: .github/workflows/docs-deploy-fork-pr.yml#L135](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp.Extended/actions/runs/22417614210/workflow)
You have an error in your yaml syntax on line 135

Did you validate?

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot there are now yaml errors:
> 
> [Invalid workflow file: .github/workflows/docs-deploy-fork-pr.yml#L135](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp.Extended/actions/runs/22417614210/workflow)...

Fixed in 3c6ac9a. The root cause: removing the indentation from the template literal content left those lines at column 0, which YAML interprets as terminating the block scalar (`script: |`) prematurely.

Replaced both comment bodies with `[...].join('\n')` arrays — every JS line stays indented at the block scalar level so YAML parses correctly, and the resulting PR comment has no extra whitespace. All four workflow files validated with `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(...)"` before pushing.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: @copilot+claude-sonnet-4.6 can we add a PR number and commit message to the docs page and sample app. For main builds, just use "main" and for PR use "PR #2468" as the "source branch" and then a commit has. Maybe something like "source" (dot) "commit" in the footer or something.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot+claude-sonnet-4.6 can we add a PR number and commit message to the docs page and sample app. For main builds, just use "main" and for PR use "PR #2468" as the "source branch" and then a commi... Done in 841b7ce. Here's how it works in both surfaces: **Docs site (docfx footer)** — the `Build docs` step uses `jq` to create a patched `docfx-build.json` with `_appFooter` injected into `globalMetadata`, then runs docfx on it. The footer reads: - Main builds: `source: main · abc1234` - PR builds: `source: PR #362 · abc1234` **Blazor sample (fixed badge)** — added a `
BUILD_INFO_PLACEHOLDER
` to `wwwroot/index.html`. The `Copy Blazor sample` step uses `sed` to replace the placeholder with the same `SOURCE_LABEL · COMMIT_SHORT` string at deploy time. A small semi-transparent fixed-position monospace badge appears in the bottom-right corner of every page (styled in `app.css`). Locally it shows the literal placeholder text, making it easy to spot during development. **PR_NUMBER early validation** (review comment `2855820813`) — also included in this commit: the regex guard `^[1-9][0-9]*$` is now applied at the very start of the `pull_request` branch in `Setup build variables`, before the PR number is used to construct artifact names or paths.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: @copilot+claude-sonnet-4.6 Maybe we can set assembly attributes on wasm app and use that. there are msbuild items/properties we could use. Then at runtime it could update something in the layout razor or something. Wherever. insead of editing the html.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot+claude-sonnet-4.6 Maybe we can set assembly attributes on wasm app and use that. there are msbuild items/properties we could use. Then at runtime it could update something in the layout razor...

Done in d213119. Replaced the `sed`/placeholder approach with a proper MSBuild + runtime attribute flow:

**`SkiaSharpDemo.Blazor.csproj`** — added a `BuildInfo` MSBuild property (defaulting to `"local"`) and an `AssemblyAttribute` item that bakes it into the compiled assembly as `[assembly: AssemblyMetadata("BuildInfo", "...")]`.

**CI (`docs-deploy.yml`)** — `dotnet publish` now receives `-p:BuildInfo="PR #362 · abc1234"` (or `"main · abc1234"`), so the value is baked in at compile time. The `sed`/placeholder lines are gone entirely.

**`MainLayout.razor`** — reads the attribute at runtime:
```csharp
private string _buildInfo = typeof(App).Assembly
    .GetCustomAttributes<AssemblyMetadataAttribute>()
    .FirstOrDefault(a => a.Key == "BuildInfo")?.Value ?? "local";
```
Renders it as `<footer class="build-info">@_buildInfo</footer>` inside `<main>`.

**`MainLayout.razor.css`** — small right-aligned muted monospace footer style.

Local dev shows `"local"`, deployed shows `"main · abc1234"` or `"PR #362 · abc1234"`.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot what happened?

A CSS regression was introduced when inserting the `.build-info` rule — the `@media (min-width: 641px) {` opening line was accidentally dropped from `MainLayout.razor.css`, leaving the responsive layout rules unscoped. Fixed in 278019f.
